title: SQL vs NoSQL Comparison
content:
### SQL vs NoSQL Comparison

NoSQL databases emerged to handle data models and scalability requirements that traditional SQL databases handle less efficiently.

---

### SQL (Relational) Databases

| Feature | SQL Databases |
|---------|---------------|
| Data model | Tables with rows and columns |
| Schema | Fixed, predefined schema |
| Relationships | Foreign keys, joins |
| ACID | Full ACID compliance |
| Scalability | Vertical (scale up) |
| Examples | PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQL Server, Oracle |

### NoSQL Database Types

| Type | Description | Examples |
|------|-------------|----------|
| Document | JSON-like documents | MongoDB, CouchDB |
| Key-Value | Simple key-value pairs | Redis, DynamoDB |
| Column-Family | Wide-column stores | Cassandra, HBase |
| Graph | Nodes and edges | Neo4j, ArangoDB |

### When to Use SQL

| Scenario | Reason |
|----------|--------|
| Structured data with clear relationships | SQL excels at joins and normalization |
| Transactions (e.g., banking) | ACID compliance ensures data integrity |
| Complex queries and reporting | SQL is powerful for ad-hoc analysis |
| Data integrity is critical | Constraints enforce rules at database level |

### When to Use NoSQL

| Scenario | Reason |
|----------|--------|
| Rapid prototyping | Schema-less, flexible data model |
| Large-scale data (Big Data) | Horizontal scaling is easier |
| Real-time applications | Low latency reads/writes |
| Unstructured or semi-structured data | Flexible document model |
| High-velocity data (logs, metrics) | Write-optimized design |

### NewSQL

NewSQL databases aim to combine SQL's ACID guarantees with NoSQL's scalability:

| Database | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| CockroachDB | Distributed SQL with horizontal scaling |
| Google Spanner | Globally distributed relational database |
| YugabyteDB | High-performance distributed SQL |

> **Key Point:** SQL and NoSQL are complementary technologies. Choose SQL for structured data with relationships and integrity requirements. Choose NoSQL for flexible schemas and horizontal scaling needs.

quiz.question: Which type of database typically provides full ACID compliance?
When is NoSQL typically preferred over SQL?
options: For flexible schemas and horizontal scaling
